title: Wealth, fertility and adaptive behaviour in industrial populations
venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B Biological Sciences
outcome: fertility (number of children) in industrial populations
reason: Reviews evolutionary explanations for why wealth and fertility have become decoupled in modern industrial populations.

title: The effects of sex and childlessness on the association between status and reproductive output in modern society
reason: Title indicates a status-reproductive-output dissociation study moderated by sex and childlessness in modern society, classic Pérusse-type territory.

title: Can Evolutionary Psychology Explain Reproductive Behavior in the Contemporary United States?
outcome: number of sex partners and coital frequency vs. number of children
reason: Replicates Perusse's dissociation test: wealthy men have more sex partners and higher coital frequency but not more children, directly evidencing the sex-reproduction decoupling under modern contraception.

title: Is Fertility Behavior in Our Genes? Findings from a Danish Twin Study
authors: Hans‐Peter Kohler; Joseph Lee Rodgers; Kaare Christensen
outcome: fertility across natural- vs controlled-fertility birth cohorts
reason: Danish twin study contrasts genetic influence on fertility/motivation in a natural-fertility cohort (1870-1910) vs a deliberately-controlled-fertility cohort (1953-64), a Pérusse-type test of how contraceptive availability changes the link between an underlying predictor and realized fertility.

title: More Status or More Children? Social Status, Fertility Reduction, and Long-Term Fitness
authors: James L. Boone; Karen L. Kessler
reason: Title directly examines whether status trades off against realized fertility and long-term fitness, the classic proximate-vs-ultimate dissociation test.

title: Childbearing motivation and its measurement
reason: Develops and validates a childbearing-motivation instrument distinct from sexual motivation, matching the motivation-distinctness estimand.

title: Ecological demography: A synthetic focus in evolutionary anthropology
venue: Evolutionary Anthropology Issues News and Reviews
reason: Evolutionary-anthropology synthesis explicitly distinguishing status-driven sexual access from reproductive success across pre-industrial and demographic-transition societies, matching the proximate/ultimate dissociation estimand.

title: Heritable and environmental variables affect reproduction-related behaviors, but not ultimate reproductive success
treatment: heritable and environmental variation in reproduction-related behaviors
reason: Title indicates a Perusse-type dissociation test: behavior-genetic variables predict reproduction-related behaviors but not actual reproductive success.

title: Resources, reproduction, and mate competition in human populations
reason: Title and evolutionary-anthropology framing directly match the proximate/ultimate resources-mating-reproduction test central to B.1, though the abstract is unavailable.

title: I Do Not Need a Second Child, But My Child Needs a Sibling: A Grounded Theory Study on the Fertility Desire for the Second Child in Chinese Post One-Child Policy Era
outcome: fertility desire for a second child (qualitative motive themes)
reason: Grounded-theory study of the content and determinants of childbearing desire, matching the motivation-distinctness/desire-for-children cell.

title: Sociosexual orientation, life history strategy, and reproductive success: A large-scale cross-cultural analysis across 92 contemporary societies
authors: Dmitry Grigoryev; Albina Gallyamova; Albina Gallyamova; Elizaveta Komyaginskaya; Elizaveta Komyaginskaya; Oulmann Zerhouni; Oulmann Zerhouni; Fanny Lalot; Fanny Lalot; Laith Al-Shawaf; Laith Al-Shawaf; Linda H. Lidborg
reason: Cross-cultural test of whether sociosexual/mating orientation translates into actual reproductive success, bearing on sexual-versus-reproductive motivation dissociation.

title: What do we need kids for? Childbearing motivations, personal values, and socio-demographic differences
outcome: childbearing motivations (four motive clusters) and their link to personal values
reason: Directly studies childbearing motivations as a distinct psychological construct, matching the motivation-distinctness cell's childbearing-motivation/desire-for-children work.

title: A matter of time: Bateman's principles and mating success as count and duration across social strata in contemporary Finland
authors: Linus Andersson; Marika Jalovaara; Jan Saarela; Caroline Uggla
venue: Proceedings of the Royal Society B Biological Sciences
treatment: mating success (mate count, cumulated union duration)
reason: Tests Bateman's principles (mating success predicting reproductive success) using contemporary Finnish register data, a modern contracepting population, finding the mating-success-to-fertility link is weakened/altered (e.g., multiple mates associated with lower reproductive success) -- a proximate-ultimate dissociation test in a post-contraception setting.

title: A Double-Track Pathway to Fast Strategy in Humans and Its Personality Correlates
authors: Fernando Gutiérrez; Josep M. Peri; Eva Baillés; B Sureda; Miguel Gárriz; Gemma Vall; Myriam Cavero; Aïda Mallorquí; José Ruiz Rodríguez
outcome: reproductive effort (offspring count, age at reproduction) vs sociosexuality (mating outcome)
reason: Tests two ostensibly linked life-history axes -- reproductive effort and sociosexual/mating behavior -- and finds them independent, a mating-vs-reproduction dissociation pattern.

title: A matter of time: Bateman’s principles and mating success as count and duration in contemporary Finland
authors: Linus Andersson; Marika Jalovaara; Jan Saarela; Caroline Uggla
reason: Bateman-type dissociation test using contemporary Finnish register data comparing mating success to reproductive success, finding the association weak/negative once union duration is adjusted for.

title: A meta-analysis of the association between male dimorphism and fitness outcomes in humans
authors: Linda H Lidborg; Catharine Penelope Cross; Lynda G Boothroyd
treatment: masculine traits (voice pitch, height, testosterone, strength/muscularity, facial masculinity, digit ratio)
reason: Meta-analysis testing whether evolutionary predictors of masculinity/mating effort translate into mating success versus actual reproductive outcomes, a Perusse-type proximate-ultimate dissociation test.

title: Childbearing motivation at the onset of emerging adulthood
outcome: desire/motivation for children among emerging adults
reason: Empirically studies childbearing motivation and desire for children as a distinct psychological construct in teens, matching the desire-for-children evidence class named in the rubric.

title: Estudio transcultural Brasil-Perú sobre las motivaciones para la parentalidad y sus variables predictoras
authors: Giuliana Violeta Vásquez Varas; Juliane Callegaro Borsa
reason: cross-cultural study of predictors of parenthood motivation, i.e. desire-for-children/childbearing-motivation work

title: Husband’s income, wife’s income, and number of biological children in the U.S.
reason: Tests status (income) against number of biological children fathered/borne, finding the status-fertility relationship persists in a post-transition society -- a Perusse-type proximate/ultimate test.

title: Social Status Predicts Different Mating and Reproductive Outcomes for Men and Women in China: Evidence from the 2010-2017 CGSS Data
outcome: mating outcome vs. reproductive outcome (gender-differentiated, post-contraception China)
reason: Title directly tests status predicting divergent mating vs. reproductive outcomes, a proximate-ultimate dissociation test.

title: Social status predicts different mating and reproductive success for men and women in China: evidence from the 2010–2017 CGSS data
outcome: mating success vs. reproductive success (childlessness, parity)
reason: Evolutionary-psychology test of status predicting mating vs. reproductive success separately, matching the Pérusse-type dissociation design.

title: What a (Young) Woman Wants: Concurrent Effects of Desire to Avoid Pregnancy and Desire for Sex on Sexual Intercourse and Contraceptive Use
treatment: desire for sex vs. desire to avoid pregnancy
reason: Weekly panel study showing desire for sex and desire to avoid pregnancy are distinct, sometimes competing motivations shaping sexual/contraceptive behavior, directly bearing on the distinctness of sexual vs. reproductive motivation.

title: Contemporary selection pressures in modern societies? Which factors best explain variance in human reproduction and mating?
outcome: Variance in human reproduction and mating in modern (contemporary selection-pressure) societies
reason: Title directly asks which evolutionary factors best explain variance in reproduction versus mating in modern societies, matching the proximate/ultimate dissociation estimand; abstract empty but title is unambiguous.

title: Creativity, Mating, and Reproductive Successes Outside the WEIRD World
authors: Izabela Lebuda; Piotr Sorokowski; Agata Groyecka-Bernard; Michalina Marczak; Aleksandra Gajda; Dorota Maria Jankowska; Maciej Karwowski
outcome: number of spouses (mating outcome) vs number of offspring (reproductive outcome)
reason: Tests whether creativity's effect on reproductive success is mediated by mating success in a natural-fertility-control (non-contracepting) population, a mating-vs-reproduction dissociation design contrasted with WEIRD populations.

title: Is Reproductive Orientation Associated with Sexual Satisfaction Among Partnered U.S. Women?
authors: Karina M. Shreffler; Larry Gibbs; Stacy Tiemeyer; Julia McQuillan; Arthur L. Greil
reason: Title directly examines the relationship between reproductive orientation and sexual satisfaction, bearing on the distinctness of reproductive vs. sexual motivation.

title: Long-term mating positively predicts both reproductive fitness and parental investment
outcome: reproductive fitness (offspring/grand-offspring number) and parental investment
treatment: long-term vs short-term mating effort (partner duration, number of sexual partners)
reason: Tests whether an evolutionary predictor of reproduction (mating effort) still predicts realized reproductive success in a modern post-industrial (contraception-available) population.

title: Predictor Variables of Childbearing Motivations in Brazilian Women and Men
authors: Giuliana Violeta Vásquez Varas; Juliane Callegaro Borsa
reason: Examines psychological/sociodemographic predictors of childbearing motivation, the desire-for-children construct central to motivation-distinctness work.

title: Warren Miller y el Modelo T-D-I-B de toma de decisiones reproductivas: Una revisión teórica
authors: Giuliana Violeta Vásquez Varas; Juliane Callegaro Borsa
reason: Theoretical review of Warren Miller's T-D-I-B model of reproductive decisions, a childbearing-motivation/desire-for-children framework bearing on motivation distinctness.

title: Childbearing Motivation Questionnaire
authors: Giuliana Violeta Vásquez Varas; Juliane Callegaro Borsa
reason: Validates a childbearing-motivation questionnaire distinguishing positive/negative motives for children, bearing on the distinctness of reproductive motivation.

title: Effects of wife’s and husband’s income on wife’s reproduction: an evolutionary perspective on human mating
outcome: wife's number of children / probability of childlessness
reason: Tests the evolutionary prediction that status/wealth predicts reproduction using modern cross-country census data, a proximate-vs-ultimate dissociation test in a contraception-available era.

title: Is male dimorphism under sexual selection in humans? A meta-analysis
authors: Linda H. Lidborg; Catharine Cross; Lynda G. Boothroyd
treatment: masculine traits (voice pitch, height, testosterone, strength/muscularity)
reason: Meta-analyzes whether evolutionarily-favored masculine traits predict mating success separately from reproductive/offspring outcomes, a mating-vs-reproduction dissociation test.

title: Is male dimorphism under sexual selection in humans? A meta-analysis
authors: Linda H. Lidborg; Lynda G. Boothroyd
outcome: mating outcomes vs reproductive/offspring viability outcomes
treatment: masculine traits (voice pitch, height, digit ratio, testosterone, strength)
reason: Meta-analysis of evolutionary predictors distinguishing mating success from reproductive outcomes.

title: Career and Family Decisions: Cohorts Born 1935-1975
authors: Zvi Eckstein; Michael P. Keane; Osnat Lifshitz
treatment: contraception technology (one of four exogenous factors)
reason: Life-cycle structural model explicitly assumes fixed preferences and quantifies contraception technology's independent contribution to fertility/marriage changes across cohorts.

title: Fertility Desires of Childless Poles: Which Childbearing Motives Matter for Men and Women?
reason: Direct study of childbearing-motivation determinants of the desire for children among childless Poles, matching the motivation-distinctness estimand.

title: Childbearing Motivations and Desires, Fertility Beliefs, and Contraceptive Use among Urban African-American Adolescents and Young Adults with STI Histories
authors: Kamila A. Alexander; Nancy Perrin; Jacky M. Jennings; Jonathan M. Ellen; Maria Trent
reason: Title explicitly studies childbearing motivations and desires alongside contraceptive use, matching the motivation-distinctness construct.

title: IS FERTILITY AFTER THE DEMOGRAPHIC TRANSITION MALADAPTIVE?
reason: Tests whether status/wealth predicts fertility differently by sex after the demographic transition, an evolutionary-predictor-vs-fertility-outcome analysis.

title: Number of Childbearing Partners, Status, and the Fertility of Men and Women in the U.S.
outcome: number of childbearing partners and fertility
reason: Tests the evolutionary prediction that status/resources differentially predict mating (childbearing partners) versus realized fertility for men and women in the contemporary U.S., a Perusse-type proximate/ultimate dissociation test.

title: Sex Differences in the Effects of Personal Resources, Family Resources, and Multiple Partners on Fertility
treatment: personal resources, family resources, multiple partners
reason: Title indicates a sex-differentiated test of evolutionary predictors (resources, mating effort proxied by multiple partners) on fertility, matching the Perusse-type dissociation design even without an abstract.

title: The Power of Abortion Policy: Reexamining the Effects of Young Women’s Access to Reproductive Control
outcome: sexual behavior vs family formation (marriage/motherhood timing)
reason: Diff-in-diff shows pill access raised sexual activity without a corresponding change in family formation, testing dissociation of sex from reproductive/family outcomes.

title: An implicit ambivalence-indifference dimension of childbearing desires in the National Survey of Family Growth
authors: Warren B. Miller; Jo Jones; David J. Pasta
reason: Directly measures the desire-for-children construct (ambivalence/indifference dimension), matching the childbearing-motivation cell.

title: Men’s status and reproductive success in 33 nonindustrial societies: Effects of subsistence, marriage system, and reproductive strategy
authors: Christopher von Rueden; Adrian V. Jaeggi
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
outcome: reproductive success (mating success, fertility, offspring mortality)
reason: Meta-analysis testing an evolutionary status predictor against mating-success versus reproductive-success outcomes, the classic proximate-ultimate dissociation design.

title: Relationship of motivation for motherhood with some sociodemographic variables and gender identity
outcome: Motivation for motherhood and its sociodemographic/gender-identity correlates
reason: Desire-for-children/childbearing-motivation survey work bearing on reproductive motivation as a psychological construct.

title: The effects of resource availability and the demographic transition on the genetic correlation between number of children and grandchildren in humans
outcome: genetic correlation between children and grandchildren counts
treatment: resource availability and demographic transition (pre/post contraception)
reason: Title indicates a test of whether genetic control of fertility persists across the demographic transition, a classic evolutionary predictor-vs-realized-fertility dissociation design, even though the abstract is missing.

title: The evolved psychological mechanisms of fertility motivation: hunting for causation in a sea of correlation
authors: Lisa McAllister; Gillian Pepper; Sandra Virgo; David A. Coall
venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B Biological Sciences
reason: Reviews the psychological mechanisms underlying human fertility motivation and decisions, directly bearing on whether reproductive motivation is a distinct psychological construct.

title: Assessment of the Relationship Between the Use of Birth Control Pill and the Characteristics of Mate Selection
authors: Alessio Gori; Marco Giannini; Giuseppe Craparo; Vincenzo Caretti; Ilaria Nannini; Renee Madathil; David Schuldberg
reason: Compares pill users vs. non-users on cycle-linked mate-preference indicators, a dissociation test of contraception's effect on an evolved mating-psychology predictor.

title: Childbearing motivational patterns of primiparous women of advanced age and their partners
venue: Journal of Reproductive and Infant Psychology
reason: Uses a Childbearing Motivations Scale to characterize distinct reproductive-motivation patterns, directly bearing on the desire-for-children construct.

title: Ecological variation in wealth–fertility relationships in Mongolia: the ‘central theoretical problem of sociobiology’ not a problem after all?
venue: Proceedings of the Royal Society B Biological Sciences
reason: Tests the wealth-fertility relationship in Mongolia as a sociobiological dissociation problem, finding selection favors status enhancement over fertility maximization -- a Perusse-type proximate/ultimate test.

title: Innovations and Implications of Life Style Drugs in an Aging Population 1
reason: Uses Viagra's introduction as a natural experiment testing whether an exogenous increase in sexual-activity capability affects natality, finding dissociation between the sex-enabling treatment and fertility.

title: Pomiar motywacji do posiadania dzieci wśród osób bezdzietnych. Polska adaptacja Kwestionariusza Motywów Rodzicielskich
outcome: Motivation to have children (parental motives) among childless adults
reason: Psychometric adaptation of a Parental Motives Questionnaire measuring desire-for-children as a distinct construct.

title: Sex differences in the relationship between status and number of offspring in the contemporary U.S.
reason: Empty abstract but title is a direct status-vs-offspring-number sex-differences study in the contemporary (post-contraception) US, matching the Perusse-type proximate/ultimate dissociation estimand.

title: THE POWER OF THE LITTLE BLUE PILL: INNOVATIONS AND IMPLICATIONS OF LIFESTYLE DRUGS IN AN AGING POPULATION
treatment: Viagra introduction (change in capacity/frequency for sexual activity)
reason: Natural experiment testing whether an exogenous change in a determinant of sexual activity (Viagra availability) shifts natality, finding no significant natality effect -- a direct test of the sex-reproduction dissociation.

title: Trends in Ages at Key Reproductive Transitions in the United States, 1951–2010
outcome: widening interval between age at first sex and age at first birth
reason: Documents a lengthening gap between first sex and first childbearing attributable to contraceptive use, a direct empirical decoupling metric.

title: An evolutionary approach to change of status–fertility relationship in human fertility transition
reason: Life-history records spanning the Finnish fertility transition testing how status-related differentials in marriage/mating relate to differentials in lifetime fertility, a Perusse-type proximate-ultimate dissociation test.

title: Can technological change account for the sexual revolution
outcome: marriage and birth-rate declines via the sexual revolution
reason: Structural model in which better contraception severs sex from unwanted-parenthood risk, driving the sexual revolution and explaining marriage/birth-rate decline.

title: Childbearing Motivations Scale: Construction of a New Measure and its Preliminary Psychometric Properties
authors: Maryse Guedes; Marco Pereira; Raquel Pires; Paula Saraiva Carvalho; María Cristina Canavarro
venue: Journal of Child and Family Studies
reason: Construction/validation of a Childbearing Motivations Scale, directly bearing on measuring reproductive motivation as a distinct psychological construct.

title: Desenvolvimento e Validação Factorial da Escala de Motivos face à Parentalidade
reason: develops and validates a factorial scale of motives for having children, i.e. desire-for-children/childbearing-motivation instrument work

title: Baby longing and men’s reproductive motivation
outcome: baby longing / reproductive motivation intensity by sex
reason: Directly measures childbearing-motivation ('baby longing') as a distinct psychological construct and its demographic effects, core to the motivation-distinctness cell.

title: Childlessness drives the sex difference in the association between income and reproductive success of modern Europeans
authors: Julia A. Barthold; Mikko Myrskylä; Owen R. Jones
reason: Tests income-reproductive success association in modern (contracepting) Europeans with childlessness as key driver, a Pérusse-type dissociation test.

title: Facial attractiveness and fertility in populations with low levels of modern birth control
authors: Antonio S. Silva; Virpi Lummaa; Ulrich Müller; Michel Raymond; Alexandra Alvergne
outcome: fertility in populations with low modern birth-control use
reason: Tests an evolutionary mating-attractiveness predictor against realized fertility specifically in populations with low modern contraceptive prevalence, the classic pre-contraception baseline for a proximate/ultimate dissociation test.

title: Natural and sexual selection in a monogamous historical human population
authors: Alexandre Courtiol; Jenni E. Pettay; Markus Jokela; Anna Rotkirch; Virpi Lummaa
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
outcome: mating success vs reproductive success (selection differentials)
reason: Decomposes natural vs sexual selection into mating-success and reproductive-success components in a historical monogamous population, an evolutionary-predictor/sexual-vs-reproductive-outcome test.

title: Childbearing motivations, pregnancy desires, and perceived partner response to a pregnancy among urban female youth: does HIV-infection status make a difference?
authors: Sarah Finocchario‐Kessler; Michael Sweat; Jacinda K. Dariotis; Jean Anderson; Jacky M. Jennings; Jean Keller; Amita A. Vyas; Maria Trent
reason: Childbearing-motivation/pregnancy-desire survey work bearing on reproductive motivation as a distinct construct.

title: Early History of Oral Contraceptive Pill in Finland: The Diffusion of the New Contraceptive and Fertility Patterns
treatment: diffusion of the oral contraceptive pill
reason: Directly examines whether pill diffusion coincided with/caused Finland's fertility decline, though causal connection explicitly not established.

title: Emotional regulation of fertility decision making: What is the nature and structure of “baby fever”?
authors: Gary L. Brase; Sandra L. Brase
outcome: desire for a baby ("baby fever") vs. general sexual desire
reason: Empirically distinguishes desire for a child from general sexual desire, evidencing distinctness of reproductive vs. sexual motivation.

title: Physical attractiveness influences reproductive success of modern men
reason: Title reports attractiveness predicting reproductive success in modern (post-contraception) men, a Perusse-type dissociation test.

title: The Impact of Emergency Contraception on Dating and Marriage
outcome: premarital sexual activity, STI rates, marriage timing, birth/single-motherhood rates
treatment: OTC access to emergency contraception (state policy natural experiment)
reason: Models and tests how improved contraceptive access severs pregnancy risk from sexual activity, increasing sex/STIs while shifting marriage and birth outcomes -- the decoupling mechanism itself.

title: Serial monogamy increases reproductive success in men but not in women
authors: Markus Jokela; Anna Rotkirch; Ian J. Rickard; Jenni E. Pettay; Virpi Lummaa
reason: Tests whether mating success (spouse turnover) translates into reproductive success in a modern contracepting US population, a Pérusse-type dissociation test.

title: Socioeconomic status, education, and reproduction in modern women: An evolutionary perspective
authors: Susanne Huber; Fred L. Bookstein; Martin Fieder
reason: Tests evolutionary predictors of fertility against modern reproductive output, framed explicitly as an evolutionary account of the demographic-transition mismatch.

title: The Contraceptive Revolution and the Second Demographic Transition: An Economic Model of Sex, Fertility, and Marriage
outcome: sexual activity and fertility (ambiguous effect), plus marriage/divorce rates
reason: Structural economic model in which higher contraceptive efficacy raises sexual activity but has ambiguous effects on realized fertility, directly modeling the sex-reproduction decoupling behind the Second Demographic Transition.

title: Low fertility in contemporary humans and the mate value of their children: sex-specific effects on social status indicators
authors: Ralf Kaptijn; Fleur Thomése; T.G. van Tilburg; Aart C. Liefbroer; Dorly J. H. Deeg
reason: Title indicates an evolutionary dissociation test between status/mate-value and realized fertility in contemporary low-fertility humans (Perusse-type proximate-ultimate test); abstract absent.

title: Social and biological determinants of reproductive success in Swedish males and females born 1915–1929
reason: Examines social and biological determinants of reproductive success in a Swedish cohort spanning the contraceptive era, fitting the proximate/ultimate dissociation genre.

title: Fertility trends by social status
outcome: fertility trends across centuries and regions
reason: Reviews the status-fertility relationship across centuries and regions, the classic evolutionary-predictor-vs-realized-fertility dissociation test central to the pre/post-contraception decoupling literature.

title: The measurement of childbearing motivation in couples considering the use of assisted reproductive technology
authors: Warren B. Miller; Susan G. Millstein; David J. Pasta
outcome: childbearing motivation subscales (desire for parenthood, genetic relatedness, pregnancy experience)
reason: Psychometric decomposition of childbearing/desire-for-children motivation among ART-seeking couples, matching the rubric's explicit desire-for-children-work example under motivation distinctness.

title: All that she wants is a(nother) baby’? Longing for children as a fertility incentive of growing importance
reason: Directly examines whether an evolved desire for children exists and how it differs from mate-selection/hormonal drives, bearing on reproductive-vs-sexual motivation distinctness.

title: Demographic effects of the introduction of steroid contraception in developed countries
reason: Directly tests the causal link between pill diffusion and fertility decline at the country level, finding no systematic correlation and attributing effects mainly to reduced unwanted pregnancies and norm change.

title: An  Empirical Test of a Possible Solution to “the Central Theoretical Problem of Human Sociobiology”
venue: Journal of Cultural and Evolutionary Psychology
treatment: intelligence / ability to use evolutionarily novel modern contraception
reason: Tests Kanazawa's hypothesis that modern contraception decouples sexual activity from reproduction more strongly among the more intelligent, while explicitly finding no class/intelligence difference in desired number of children (GSS data).

title: Money and Sex, The Illusory Universal Sex Difference: Comment on Kanazawa
outcome: number of sex partners / coital frequency
reason: Re-examines Kanazawa's Perusse-type evolutionary test of income/status predicting sexual (copulatory) behavior rather than reproduction.

title: Status and Reproduction in Humans: New Evidence for the Validity of Evolutionary Explanations on Basis of a University Sample
authors: Martin Fieder; Susanne Huber; Fred L. Bookstein; Karoline Iber; Katrin Schäfer; Georg Winckler; Bernard Wallner
reason: Tests the Pérusse-type dissociation of status from reproductive success in a modern (contraception-available) sample versus traditional societies.

title: CAN EVOLUTIONARY PSYCHOLOGY EXPLAIN REPRODUCTIVE BEHAVIOR IN THE CONTEMPORARY UNITED STATES?
outcome: sex partners/coital frequency vs completed fertility
reason: Replicates Perusse-type dissociation: wealthy men have more sex partners/coital frequency but not more children given contraception.

title: Succès social et succès reproductif dans les sociétés modernes : une analyse sociobiologique
reason: Sociobiological theoretical model and literature review testing whether social success still predicts reproductive success in modern societies.

title: The Motivational Substrate of Unintended and Unwanted Pregnancy1
authors: Warren B. Miller; David J. Pasta
reason: Models a biologically based childbearing-motivation substrate, conscious and unconscious, driving contraceptive behavior and pregnancy wantedness — bears on the distinctness of reproductive motivation.

title: Reproductive and Contraceptive Attitudes as Predictors of Condom Use Among Women in an HIV Prevention Intervention
authors: Rebecca J. Cabral; Christine Galavotti; Kay Armstrong; Brian Morrow; Linda Fogarty
reason: Measures childbearing motivation as a distinct psychological construct predicting contraceptive/sexual behavior (desire for a baby lowers condom-use consistency).

title: A Heritability Study of Childbearing Motivation
authors: David J. Pasta; Warren B. Miller
reason: Directly studies childbearing motivation as a heritable psychological construct, bearing on the distinctness of reproductive motivation from sexual motivation.

title: Genetic Influences on Childbearing Motivation: Further Testing a Theoretical Framework
authors: Warren B. Miller; David J. Pasta; James P. MacMurray; Donn Muhleman; David E. Comings
reason: Directly tests genetic influences on childbearing motivation, the desire-for-children construct central to MOTIVATION_DISTINCTNESS.

title: Couple childbearing desires, intentions, and births
reason: Panel study testing whether couples' childbearing desires and intentions predict births, directly probing the desire-fertility link central to the desire-independence estimand.

title: Monogamy, contraception and the cultural and reproductive success hypothesis
reason: Title directly tests a cultural-and-reproductive-success hypothesis linking monogamy and contraception to reproductive success, a Perusse-type dissociation test, though the abstract text is unavailable.

title: Motivational and nonmotivational determinants of child-number desires
authors: Warren B. Miller; David J. Pasta
reason: Title directly addresses motivational determinants of child-number desires, matching the reproductive-motivation-distinctness estimand cell even absent abstract.

title: Social dominance attainment, testosterone, libido and reproductive success
reason: Title indicates a direct test of status/testosterone/libido as evolutionary predictors against reproductive success, matching the proximate-ultimate dissociation estimand (abstract unavailable).

title: Some evidence on cultural and reproductive success in the United States
reason: Title indicates a cultural/status-predictor-of-reproductive-success study, matching the Perusse-type dissociation-test cell.

title: Status, reproductive success, and marrying polygynously
reason: Title indicates a classic evolutionary status-to-reproductive-success (mating effort) design, the Pérusse-type proximate-ultimate dissociation test.

title: The Contraceptive Pill and Women's Employment as Factors in Fertility Change in Britain 1963–1980: A Challenge to the Conventional View
treatment: diffusion of the oral contraceptive pill (incl. 'pill scares' as natural shocks)
reason: Tests pill diffusion, including sudden 'pill scare' shocks, as the main proximate determinant driving fertility decline, directly bearing on the severing technology's effect on realized fertility.

title: Personality traits and developmental experiences as antecedents of childbearing motivation
reason: Directly studies childbearing motivation as a distinct psychological construct shaped by traits and experience.

title: A model of fertility motivation, desires, and expectations early in women's reproductive careers
authors: Warren B. Miller; David J. Pasta
reason: Structural model treating childbearing motivation as a distinct antecedent construct from desires and expectations, matching the motivation-distinctness cell.

title: Reproductive success and occupational class in eighteenth‐century lancashire, England
reason: Classic evolutionary-demography test of status predicting reproductive success in a pre-contraceptive population.

title: Sexual strategies and social-class differences in fitness in modern industrial societies
outcome: fitness (reproductive success) in modern industrial societies
reason: Examines whether social-class-linked sexual strategies still translate into reproductive fitness in modern contracepting societies, a Pérusse-type dissociation test.

title: The reproductive success of wealthy Americans
reason: Classic sociobiological study testing whether wealth/status predicts reproductive success among Americans in a modern, contracepted society (Pérusse-type dissociation territory).
